findings.title: Claude reported closeout validation gate does not parse
findings.adjudication_detail: Live verification showed line 1746 is valid JavaScript, rg found no EOF marker/dead tail strings, and node --check scripts/validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs exited 0 before and after remediation.

findings.title: Claude reported dead SUBPHASE_TO_FIXTURE array bloat
findings.adjudication_detail: Live rg found no SUBPHASE_TO_FIXTURE, EOF marker, or trailing dead-tail marker in the validator script. The finding does not apply to the live repo.

findings.title: Permission-precheck policy validator lacked exact failClosedOn length guard
findings.adjudication_detail: Added policy.failClosedOn.length !== 15 drift guard and service test assertion for the S05 policy failClosedOn length; targeted syntax and 443 service tests pass after remediation.

security_notes: Claude found the S05 policy/service/fixture values internally consistent and the substantive S05 logic sound.
Valid P2 remediation strengthens fail-closed policy drift detection for permission precheck failClosedOn reasons.
test_notes: node --check scripts/validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs was rerun after adjudication and passed.
raw_review.summary: Raw Claude result requested changes, but two findings were rejected by direct live repo verification and the one valid P2 was fixed before closeout.

raw_review_text:
I have enough to finalize. The contract/policy/fixture/service values align (and the 443-test suite covers the policy↔service↔fixture triangle via `deepEqual`), so the substantive S05 logic is sound. The decisive problem is the closeout gate itself.

## Summary

The S05 permission-precheck logic (policy/service/validator/fixture) is internally consistent and well-tested. The blocking problem is that the **closeout validation gate script does not parse**, which makes the pack's stated validation evidence false. Verified directly: `node --check scripts/validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs` exits 1 with `SyntaxError: Unexpected token '#'` at line 1746.

```json
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "P0",
      "title": "Closeout validation gate does not parse — npm run rp00:control-plane:validate cannot run, pack evidence claims are false",
      "file": "scripts/validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs",
      "line": 1746,
      "body": "Line 1746 is `# EOF marker (not real)`, a shell-style comment that is invalid JavaScript. Verified: `node --check scripts/validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs` exits 1 with `SyntaxError: Unexpected token '#'` at 1746, so `npm run rp00:control-plane:validate` crashes at parse time before any check runs. This falsifies two of the pack's stated evidence lines: (1) `node --check ...: pass` is false; (2) `npm run rp00:control-plane:validate failed only because docs/closeout-packs/cp00-021 evidence files do not exist yet` is false — it fails on the syntax error, and the script has no evidence-file existence check at all (verifyClosureState only asserts contract fields). Net: the only automated guard for contract↔policy↔fixture drift never executes (the unit tests never read the contract JSON), yet latest_closeout/current_subphase are marked production_ready. The broken tail (this line, the dead array below, `const trailing = 1;` at 1748, and `main()` invoked at 1745 before its declaration) is already in HEAD, so it was committed by a prior pack and is not literally in the CP00-021 +diff — but CP00-021 cannot legitimately close on a non-functional gate, and its evidence section misrepresents the result. Fix: remove line 1746 and the dead lines, then re-run node --check and the validator."
    },
    {
      "severity": "P2",
      "title": "~1,336-line dead SUBPHASE_TO_FIXTURE array bloats the gate script",
      "file": "scripts/validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs",
      "line": 349,
      "body": "SUBPHASE_TO_FIXTURE (lines 349–1685) is Object.freeze([...]) with two string entries followed by ~1,330 identical `permissionPrecheckExpectations` references. It is consumed only at line 1743 (`const SUBPHASE_TO_FIXTURE_UNUSED = SUBPHASE_TO_FIXTURE;`) behind an eslint-disable for no-unused-vars — pure dead code. The name implies a subphase→fixture map, but the contents are one object repeated, so it is a generation/copy-paste artifact (same corrupted tail that produced the P0 syntax error). Delete the array and its unused alias. Also pre-existing in HEAD."
    },
    {
      "severity": "P2",
      "title": "Permission-precheck policy validator guards forbiddenPermissionClaims length but not failClosedOn length",
      "file": "packages/control-plane/src/validators.js",
      "line": 9846,
      "body": "assertControlPlaneSyntheticFixtureSetPermissionPrecheckPolicy enforces an exact-length guard for forbiddenPermissionClaims (`if (policy.forbiddenPermissionClaims.length !== 26) throw`, ~9843), but the parallel failClosedOn block (~9846–9866) uses only requireArrayIncludesAll with no `policy.failClosedOn.length !== 15` guard afterward. Asymmetric drift protection: a duplicated/extra fail-closed reason could be added without the validator or any test catching it, while the same drift in forbiddenPermissionClaims is caught. The contract records fail_closed_on_count: 15, so the invariant is intended but unverified. Add a length check mirroring the forbiddenPermissionClaims guard (and ideally a policy test asserting failClosedOn length)."
    }
  ],
  "verdict": "REQUEST_CHANGES — P0: the CP00-021 closeout gate (scripts/validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs) fails node --check at line 1746, so npm run rp00:control-plane:validate cannot run, the pack's validation evidence is false, and no automated contract/policy/fixture drift check actually executes for a closeout marked production_ready. Must fix the gate (and re-validate) before close; two P2 cleanups also noted."
}
```

Note on scope: the P0 syntax error and the dead array are present in HEAD (a prior pack committed them), not in the CP00-021 +diff itself — but they block CP00-021's own gate and make its evidence claims false, so they must be resolved before this closeout can be trusted. Only finding #3 is newly introduced by the CP00-021 validator code.
adjudication_notes: Claude raw findings were adjudicated: P0 validator-parse finding rejected by live node --check and line inspection; dead-array P2 rejected by live rg; failClosedOn length-guard P2 fixed in validators.js and service.test.js. Unresolved P0/P1/P2 after adjudication is 0.
